change_summary: added a manifest-driven runtime scan contract, documented the cached `runtime_scan_summary.json` routing aid, refreshed the template root summary, and generated the first low-token section scan cache for the project template
updated the requirement template guidance so canonical system and user requirement filenames are `system_requirement.json` and `user_requirements.json`

tightened the assumptions-and-constraints template guidance so allowed statuses are type-specific: assumptions use active, validated, invalidated, or retired, while constraints use active, lifted, or retired

tightened the execution-item template guidance so statuses are limited to planned, in_progress, blocked, done, abandonned, and backlog, with done items expected to record completion dates for active versus inactive dashboard grouping
updated the roadmap template guidance so future projects can represent both current contracted scope and clearly labeled long-term vision phases, with explicit TBD markers for uncontracted horizons

clarified the system and subsystem requirement template guidance so repo-managed references keep full internal traceability while front-facing Excel reference cells only expose client or SharePoint sources, or `TBD` when no external source exists

tightened the requirement template contract so verification-specific fields stay TBD until verification work begins, legacy compliance field names were renamed, and references plus follow-up notes are now dated append-only lists
clarified governance and COTS pathing guidance in the template so folder expectations and external evidence locations match the current workspace logic
updated template COTS guidance to use component-area subfolders with one canonical JSON per area and SharePoint-hosted source captures
